description: Ethereum Mainnet Builder Codes - Replicating Base's ERC-8021 attribution system for Ethereum L1. Purely for tracking and analytics. Uses ox/erc8021 library (same as Base), an ERC-721 registry contract, Scaffold-ETH 2 frontend with native dataSuffix support (viem 2.45.0+), and Dune dashboards for analytics. No subgraph - direct RPC reads + Dune API for all data.
commitRule: After completing each user story, commit ALL changes with message: 'feat: [Story ID] - [Story Title]'. Do NOT include any co-author or attribution lines - only the commit message content itself. Commit after every story, no exceptions.

techStack.erc8021: ox/erc8021 (Attribution.toDataSuffix) - same library Base uses, DO NOT build custom
techStack.dataLayer: Direct RPC reads (getLogs for events, Multicall3 for batch reads) + Dune API for analytics. No subgraph.
techStack.analytics: Dune Analytics (DuneSQL/Trino) + Dune API for dashboard integration
techStack.testing: Foundry unit tests + Vitest for TypeScript
techStack.deployment: Foundry scripts + Anvil local + Sepolia testnet + Mainnet

keyInsights.gasReality2026: NFT mint ~$0.03, contract deploy ~$0.50 at 0.1 gwei base fee. NOT $50-100.
keyInsights.nativeDataSuffix: Viem 2.45.0+ and Wagmi support dataSuffix as a first-class config option. Set once in createConfig, auto-applied to ALL transactions.
keyInsights.oxErc8021: Base uses 'ox/erc8021' package (Attribution.toDataSuffix). Reuse it - do not build a custom codec from scratch.
keyInsights.suffixFormat: Schema byte (0x00) + code length byte + ASCII code bytes + repeating 0x8021 marker at end of calldata
keyInsights.scaffoldEth2: npx create-eth@latest gives you auto-generated TS types from contracts, built-in hooks, and IPFS deployment
keyInsights.noSubgraph: No Graph subgraph needed. Use viem getLogs for CodeMinted events (fine for <10K codes) + Multicall3 for batch reads + Dune API for analytics. Keeps infra simple.
keyInsights.singleContract: Only 1 contract needed (CodesRegistry). Purely for tracking - no payouts, no rewards, no money movement.
keyInsights.noPayout: No payout address concept. The registry maps codes to owners (via ERC-721 ownership). That's it.
keyInsights.baseAudited: Contract pattern is already audited by Base. No need for fuzz/invariant tests - basic unit tests are sufficient.

userStories.title: Foundry project setup and ERC-721 registry contract
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core CodesRegistry contract: an ERC-721 where unique ASCII codes (1-32 chars) are minted as NFTs. The owner of the NFT is the owner of that builder code. No payout addresses - purely identity/tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Foundry project in contracts/ with forge init
Install OpenZeppelin: forge install OpenZeppelin/openzeppelin-contracts
CodesRegistry.sol extends ERC721 with unique ASCII string codes (1-32 lowercase alphanumeric + underscore)
mint(string calldata code) - reverts on duplicate, invalid chars, or empty code. Mints to msg.sender.
getCode(uint256 tokenId) view returns the ASCII code string for a token
codeExists(string calldata code) view returns bool
tokenIdForCode(string calldata code) view returns uint256
ownerOfCode(string calldata code) view returns address (convenience wrapper around ownerOf + tokenIdForCode)
Event: CodeMinted(uint256 indexed tokenId, string code, address indexed owner)
Indexed topics on events for efficient log filtering
Input validation: code length bounds (1-32), ASCII-only enforcement
forge build succeeds, forge test passes with unit tests covering: mint, duplicate rejection, ownership checks, max length code, empty code revert, invalid chars revert, transfer updates ownership
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-001 - Foundry project setup and ERC-721 registry contract'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Only 1 contract needed - purely for tracking. Use string-to-tokenId mapping for O(1) lookup. Ownership is just ERC-721 ownerOf. Gas cost for mint should be ~150K gas (~$0.03 at current rates). Contract pattern already audited by Base.

userStories.title: Local Anvil deployment and gas report
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Foundry deployment scripts that work against a local Anvil instance (no real wallet needed), with a post-deploy smoke test and gas report. Production deployment (Sepolia/mainnet) is documented in a guide but NOT executed.
acceptanceCriteria: Foundry script Deploy.s.sol deploys CodesRegistry and logs contract address
Script deploys to local Anvil using Anvil's default test accounts (no real private key needed)
Anvil startup command documented: anvil (local) or anvil --fork-url https://eth.llamarpc.com (fork mode)
Post-deploy smoke test script: mints a code, queries ownerOfCode, verifies correctness - all on local Anvil
.gitignore includes .env, .env.*, broadcast/, cache/, out/
Gas report: forge test --gas-report shows mint costs ~150K gas
All forge tests still pass

README in contracts/ with: (1) Local Anvil deploy instructions (run now), (2) Production guide section covering: how to deploy to Sepolia/mainnet when ready, .env setup for RPC_URL + PRIVATE_KEY + ETHERSCAN_API_KEY, forge verify-contract for Etherscan, gas settings (maxFeePerGas: 1-2 gwei)
Production guide is documentation only - do NOT attempt real deployment or require a funded wallet
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-002 - Local Anvil deployment and gas report'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: All deployment runs locally on Anvil with test accounts. No real wallet or ETH needed. Production deploy (Sepolia/mainnet) is a future manual step - just document how.

userStories.title: Scaffold-ETH 2 frontend with wallet connection and dataSuffix config
userStories.description: As a user, I need a web frontend to connect my wallet and interact with builder codes. Use Scaffold-ETH 2 for auto-generated contract types and built-in hooks. Configure Wagmi with native dataSuffix support (viem 2.45.0+).
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize with npx create-eth@latest in apps/web/ (or project root per SE2 conventions)
Wagmi config uses createConfig with dataSuffix from ox/erc8021 Attribution.toDataSuffix
RainbowKit wallet connection: MetaMask, WalletConnect, Coinbase Wallet
Connected wallet address displayed in header (use SE2 <Address/> component pattern)
Chain config: Ethereum mainnet primary, Sepolia for testing
Contract ABI auto-generated from Foundry artifacts via SE2 tooling
Tailwind CSS responsive layout
Per-button loading states on all async operations (ethskills: 'blockchains take 5-12 seconds')
npm install ox viem@^2.45.0 wagmi - verify dataSuffix support
TypeScript strict mode, eslint passes, dev server runs clean
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-003 - Scaffold-ETH 2 frontend with wallet connection and dataSuffix config'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Key insight from Base docs: dataSuffix is a first-class Wagmi/Viem config option. Set it once in createConfig and ALL transactions auto-include the builder code. No per-tx code needed.

userStories.title: Code claiming flow - mint UI with real-time validation
userStories.description: As a builder, I want to claim a unique code by entering it, seeing real-time availability, and minting the ERC-721 NFT to my connected wallet.
acceptanceCriteria: Claim page at /claim with input for code string
Real-time validation: length (1-32), ASCII lowercase alphanumeric + underscore only
Debounced on-chain availability check via useScaffoldReadContract (codeExists)
Green check / red X availability indicator
Mint button using useScaffoldWriteContract or useWriteContract
Three-button flow pattern if needed: Switch Network → Mint (ethskills frontend guide)
Transaction lifecycle: pending spinner → confirming → confirmed with Etherscan link
Error handling: wallet rejection, insufficient funds, code taken (with clear user messages)
Success state: shows minted code, token ID, owner address, and 'View My Codes' link
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-004 - Code claiming flow - mint UI with real-time validation'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Core UX flow. Ethskills frontend: 'Display loading states on all async operations.' No payout address field needed - NFT ownership IS the identity.

userStories.title: My codes dashboard with Multicall3 batch reads
userStories.description: As a builder, I want to see all my claimed codes and their details. Use Multicall3 for efficient batch contract reads.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard page at /my-codes showing all codes owned by connected wallet
Fetch owned token IDs via getLogs on Transfer events (from zero address = mints to user)
Batch-read code details via Multicall3 (0xcA11bde05977b3631167028862bE2a173976CA11) - single RPC call for all codes
Each code card: code string, token ID, mint block timestamp
Loading skeleton states, empty state ('No codes yet - claim your first!')
Responsive grid layout
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-005 - My codes dashboard with Multicall3 batch reads'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Use viem getLogs to fetch Transfer events + client.multicall for batch reads. No subgraph needed - direct RPC is fine for per-user queries.

userStories.title: Transaction verification tool
userStories.description: As anyone, I want to paste a transaction hash and check if it contains an ERC-8021 builder code suffix, see which code it is, and who owns it.
acceptanceCriteria: Verification page at /verify
Input field for Ethereum tx hash with format validation
Fetch tx via viem getTransaction (public client)
Decode suffix using ox/erc8021 Attribution parsing (same as Base's checker)
If found: display builder code, look up owner from registry contract
If not found: clear 'No builder code found' message
Link to Etherscan for the transaction
Handle: invalid hash, tx not found, network errors
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-006 - Transaction verification tool'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Based on Base's builder-code-checker (builder-code-checker.vercel.app). Uses ox/erc8021 for decoding - same library, just pointing at mainnet.

userStories.title: Documentation page - full system explainer and developer integration guide
userStories.description: As a visitor or developer, I need a comprehensive /docs page that explains what Ethereum Builder Codes are, how the entire system works end-to-end, the ERC-8021 standard, and how to integrate attribution into any dApp.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs page at /docs with sidebar navigation between sections
Section 1 - What are Builder Codes: explains the concept, why attribution matters, how Base pioneered it, and how this brings it to mainnet
Section 2 - How it works: end-to-end flow diagram (Claim code → Integrate suffix → Transactions tagged → Dune tracks everything)

Section 3 - ERC-8021 standard: visual byte format diagram [schema 0x00][length byte][ASCII code bytes][0x8021 repeating marker], explanation that smart contracts ignore trailing calldata, gas cost per suffix (< $0.001)
Section 4 - Registry contract: what the ERC-721 does, mint/ownerOfCode/codeExists functions, contract address, link to Etherscan
Section 5 - Developer integration guide: quick start (npm install ox wagmi viem), one-line Wagmi config with Attribution.toDataSuffix, Viem example, per-transaction legacy example
Section 6 - Wallet compatibility: EOA wallets work natively, smart wallets via ERC-5792 DataSuffixCapability, Privy/Turnkey supported
Section 7 - Analytics and tracking: how Dune parses suffixes, link to public dashboard, how to build your own queries
Section 8 - FAQ: cost to mint, cost per tagged tx, can codes be transferred, what happens if suffix is on unsupported contract, L2 compatibility
Interactive suffix generator tool: enter your code → shows the hex suffix from Attribution.toDataSuffix
Copy-to-clipboard on all code snippets
Clean typography, responsive, linked from landing page and nav
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-007 - Documentation page - full system explainer and developer integration guide'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: This is both the 'learn about it' page AND the 'integrate it' page. Covers the full picture for non-technical visitors AND gives developers copy-paste code. Key selling point: one-line config change for any Wagmi/Viem app.

userStories.title: Dune Analytics SQL queries for mainnet attribution
userStories.description: As an analyst, I need ready-to-use DuneSQL queries that parse ERC-8021 suffixes from ethereum.transactions and produce attribution metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL files in analytics/dune/ directory
Query 1: Parse transactions with 0x8021 suffix - scan last bytes of 'data' column for repeating 0x8021 marker, extract code
Query 2: Join parsed codes with CodesRegistry CodeMinted events to get code owners
Query 3: Daily volume (ETH value), gas fees (gas_used * gas_price), unique users per code
Query 4: Top 50 codes by 28-day attributed transaction count and volume
Query 5: Leaderboard ranked by total fees generated (SUM of gas_used * effective_gas_price)
Query 6: All registered codes with owners (replaces subgraph for explorer data)
All queries in DuneSQL/Trino syntax (not Spark SQL)
Inline comments explaining suffix parsing logic
README: how to create these dashboards on dune.com, how to use Dune API (api.dune.com) to fetch results
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-008 - Dune Analytics SQL queries for mainnet attribution'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Dune handles both analytics AND registry browsing. Query 6 provides the all-codes list that would have come from a subgraph. Dune API serves it to the frontend.

userStories.title: Public analytics dashboard with Dune API integration
userStories.description: As a community member, I want a public analytics page showing live builder code tracking metrics with data from Dune API.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics page at /analytics
Summary stats cards: total codes minted, total attributed txs, total fees tracked
Leaderboard table: top codes by 28-day volume
Volume trend line chart (daily granularity) using recharts
Time range filter: 7d, 28d, 90d, all-time
Data fetching layer with interface: fetchLeaderboard(), fetchStats(), fetchVolumeTrend()
Mock data implementation that matches Dune API response shape
Dune API integration: fetch from api.dune.com/api/v1/query/{id}/results with API key
Graceful fallback to cached/mock data when Dune API is unavailable
Each code row links to detail view
Responsive, mobile-friendly
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-009 - Public analytics dashboard with Dune API integration'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Start with mock data, design for Dune API swap. Cache Dune results (refresh every 5-15 min), don't call on every page load.

userStories.title: Off-chain metadata with EIP-712 signature verification
userStories.description: As a builder, I want to add a display name, website, and logo to my code profile. Only the code owner can update, verified via EIP-712 signed message.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js API route: GET /api/metadata/[code] returns metadata
Next.js API route: PUT /api/metadata/[code] with EIP-712 signed message body
Metadata schema: { code, displayName, website, logoUrl, description }
EIP-712 domain separator with contract address and chainId for replay protection
Verify signer is token owner by checking ownerOf on registry contract
File-based JSON storage for MVP (data/metadata/ directory)
Logo upload: max 500KB, image/* MIME types only, stored locally or external URL
Input validation: URL format, string lengths, no script injection
Metadata displayed on code cards in /my-codes, /explore, /analytics
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-010 - Off-chain metadata with EIP-712 signature verification'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Ethskills security: 'EIP-712 enables structured data signing with domain separation and replay protection.' Ethskills ship: 'User profiles stay offchain.'

userStories.title: Registry explorer with RPC event logs and Dune data
userStories.description: As anyone, I want to browse all minted builder codes with search and sorting, using direct RPC getLogs for registry data and Dune API for attribution stats.
acceptanceCriteria: Explorer page at /explore
Fetch all CodeMinted events via viem getLogs (from contract deploy block to latest)
Cache event results client-side or via Next.js API route (refresh on new mints)
Search by code name (client-side filter on cached results)
Sort by: newest, oldest (by block timestamp)
Each card: code name, owner (with ENS resolution via viem getEnsName if available), metadata from API
Click-through to code detail page with Dune-powered attribution stats
Responsive design
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-011 - Registry explorer with RPC event logs and Dune data'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: getLogs on CodeMinted events is fine for <10K codes. Cache results server-side and refresh periodically. For attribution stats on detail pages, use Dune API.

userStories.title: End-to-end integration test on Anvil
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an E2E test that deploys the registry, mints codes, sends attributed transactions with ox/erc8021 suffixes, and verifies the complete tracking flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Test script in test/e2e/ using Viem + local Anvil
Spins up Anvil instance (anvil --fork-url https://eth.llamarpc.com)
Deploys CodesRegistry via Viem
Mints 3 test codes
Generates ERC-8021 suffixes using ox/erc8021 Attribution.toDataSuffix for each code
Sends transactions with dataSuffix appended to calldata
Reads back transaction calldata and decodes suffix - verifies correct code attribution
Verifies ownerOfCode lookups return correct owners
Tests code transfer (ERC-721 transfer) and verifies new owner via ownerOfCode
All assertions pass, clean Anvil teardown
Runnable via: npm run test:e2e
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-012 - End-to-end integration test on Anvil'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Validates the entire tracking system before mainnet.

userStories.title: Landing page with ecosystem positioning
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a clear landing page explaining Ethereum Builder Codes, how they work, and why they matter - positioned as 'what Base did, now for all of Ethereum.'
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page at / with hero: 'Attribution for Ethereum. Know who built what.'
How it works: 3-step visual (1. Claim code → 2. Add one line of config → 3. Track everything)
Emphasize simplicity: 'One line of code. Every transaction attributed.'
Benefits: analytics, tracking, ecosystem visibility
Social proof: 'Built on ERC-8021, the same standard powering Base Builder Codes'
CTA buttons: Claim Your Code, Read the Docs, View Leaderboard
Technical section: ERC-8021 byte format diagram, gas cost callout ('< $0.001 per transaction')
FAQ: cost to mint (~$0.03), wallet compatibility, L2 support, contract verification
Footer: GitHub, Dune dashboard link, contract on Etherscan, ERC-8021 spec
Responsive, fast load, no heavy images
TypeScript strict, lint passes
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-013 - Landing page with ecosystem positioning'. No co-author lines.

userStories.title: Security audit checklist and Slither static analysis
userStories.description: As a developer preparing for mainnet, I need to run Slither static analysis on the registry contract and verify the ethskills security checklist passes.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and run slither . on contracts/ - zero high/medium findings
Verify: all state-changing functions have explicit access control (mint is permissionless but ownership-gated functions check ownerOf)
Verify: CEI pattern followed
Verify: all events emitted for state changes (for indexers)
Verify: no hardcoded addresses (use constructor params or immutables)
Verify: string input bounded (1-32 chars, ASCII only)
Document: gas costs for all public functions via forge test --gas-report
Document: post-deploy checklist (verify on Etherscan)
All forge tests pass, slither clean
COMMIT: git add all changes and commit with message 'feat: US-014 - Security audit checklist and Slither static analysis'. No co-author lines.
userStories.notes: Single contract with no money handling keeps attack surface minimal.
